explanation.rule: Adding -ment to verbs creates nouns that describe actions, processes, or states.

groups.note: Added to verbs to form nouns

wordForms.example: They had a heated argument over the budget.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to give reasons for or against something

wordForms.example: There has been a significant improvement in her grades.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to make something better than before

wordForms.example: The seating arrangement worked perfectly for the event.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to put things in order or plan something

wordForms.example: The political movement gained millions of supporters.

wordForms.definition: the state of having paid work
wordForms.example: Full employment remains the government's main goal.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to pay someone to do work

wordForms.example: The police asked him to make a formal statement.

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: Her teachers noticed a remarkable ___ in her writing skills.
He read out a brief ___ before leaving the room.
The ___ for the meeting was confirmed last night.
The rise in ___ has helped reduce poverty in the region.

transformExercises.sentence: The ___ over the new contract lasted for weeks. (ARGUE)
The new bridge is a major ___ for the local community. (IMPROVE)
The ___ of workers in the city has risen sharply. (EMPLOY)
